texto: Son, it’s easy tae be guid oan a fu’ belly. It’s when a man’s goat two bites an’ wan o’ them he’ll share, ye ken whit he’s made o’. Listen. In ony country in the world, who are the only folk that ken whit it’s like tae leeve in that country? The folk at the boattom. The rest can a’ kid themselves oan. They can afford to hiv fancy ideas. We canny, son. We loass the wan idea o’ who we are, we’re deid. We’re wan anither. Tae survive, we’ll respect wan anither. When the time comes, we’ll a’ move forward thegither, or nut at all.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a William McIlvanney: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Son, it’s easy tae be guid oan a fu’ belly. It’s when a man’s goat two bites an’ wan o’ them he’l…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, 1920s, politics pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de politics e scotland.
